mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding household income and consumption, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month increases fail to coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of observed months, or the mean return conditional on PAYEMS growth is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises corporate borrowing costs and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances, or the mean conditional return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) combined with a federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 2.0% reflects a non-inflationary expansion that reduces real uncertainty and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of rising PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS below 2.0% fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term does not significantly improve prediction over main effects (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 5.0%, because output gains in slack economies signal accelerating profit recovery rather than inflationary overheating.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, month-over-month increases in INDPRO do not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) falling below its trailing 3-month average signals a contraction in consumer demand that reduces expected corporate revenues and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines below its 3-month trailing average
failure_condition: Declines in RSAFS below its 3-month trailing average do not produce negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and decreases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicate a supply-side recovery supported by monetary easing, which amplifies equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases month-over-month and FEDFUNDS decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of rising INDPRO and falling FEDFUNDS does not produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) predict negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 5.0%, because tight labor markets amplify cost pass-through to wages and sharpen expectations of aggressive monetary tightening.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is positive
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0%, positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes fail to coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5% signals a rapid deterioration in residential investment and construction employment, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month HOUST declines exceeding 5% do not produce negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.
